GETTING STARTED – Intro to PLF (PLF CORE)

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Executive Summary:
• 	Product Launch Formula® has been proven by hundreds and hundreds of clients in
just about every kind of niche and every kind of market that you can imagine.
• 	I personally started developing these strategies in 1996 in a market that had nothing
to do with building your business, marketing, or entrepreneurship.
• 	After teaching a few friends about this, and I started to realize these techniques
worked in any market.
• 	In 2005, I released the very first version of Product Launch Formula, and since then
have had hundreds of clients that together have done over half a billion dollars in
sales.
• 	PLF continues to get updated, adding in the latest strategies and tactics that I learn.
• 	This is tested; it's proven. You're in the right place. This will work for you.
• 	Early on I studied the old-school direct-marketing tactics and strategies because that
was the only thing you could study back in 1996.
• 	I learned there are three core fundamentals in direct marketing: the list, the offer,
and the copy.
• 	If you got two out of three right, you’d usually be ok. If you got all three of them
working, then you win.
• 	With Product Launch Formula, we've changed this around a little bit.
• 	You're going to be building a business where you build your own list. The offer is
going to be determined by your market and the Launch Conversation you’ll have with
them (which you’ll learn about later). Because of the sequential nature of your launch,
you're able to fine-tune your copy as you go through your launch.
§ 	PLF is about community - getting involved, getting support, and giving support is the
way you win. Start by leaving a comment below the video and let me know you’re
here.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Avoid these two common mistakes:
1. 	Thinking this only works for high-priced products, for gurus, or for make-money
products, and only if you have a list.
o 	Nobody starts off as a guru. They did launches and then became known as
gurus, and you can too (if you want).
o 	My prototypical case study, John Gallagher, makes most of his sales from a
$10/month membership site so it doesn’t matter if your product is not a
high-priced product.
o 	PLF has been used in so many different markets: learning to play guitar,
health food, meditation, brain trauma, learning sports and so many others.
o 	A launch is the best way to build a list.
(The bottom line is that this is all proven and it’s going to work for you. Yes, you’re
going to learn about launches, but PLF is actually going to restructure your
business.)
2. 	Thinking “I knew that” or “I disagree”.
o 	Don’t tune out and say “I already knew that” because this will hold you back.
Don’t be “too cool for school”.
o 	Not everything will make sense to you initially but I’ve been doing this stuff
for a long time. Follow the plan and you’ll see how it works.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

TRANSCRIPT:
Welcome to the PLF Core Training. I have three videos here for you in this
Core Training. The whole idea is to give you a jump start before we get into module
one.
One of the key differences between Product Launch Formula® and many
trainings, and trust me, I go through a lot of different trainings, is that it's proven.
I've had success after success, but I have hundreds and hundreds of clients that have
had great successes in just about every kind of niche and every kind of market that
you can imagine.
I started developing these strategies when I first started my very first online
business in 1996. I spent about eight or nine years, from 1996 until 2005, using these
strategies, developing the strategies, tweaking the strategies - in a market that had
nothing to do with building your business, marketing, or entrepreneurship. I was in
the stock market, a completely different market.
Then in 2003, I started teaching a few friends about this, and I started to
realize these techniques worked in other markets besides in the stock market. Then
in 2005, I released the very first version of Product Launch Formula, and the proof's
in the pudding. We have hundreds of clients that together have done half a
billion dollars in sales.
It just crushes, and it just keeps on rolling along. It's been continually
updated; I keep on updating. I keep on adding in the latest strategies and tactics that
I learn, many of which my students bring to me. This is tested; it's proven. You're in
the right place. This will work for you.
One of the things I wanted to talk about was just the overall conceptual
aspects of Product Launch Formula. One of my successes early on was because I
realized that the online world, the Internet, was a great place to use a lot of the old-
school direct-marketing tactics and strategies - so even though I had no background
in old-school direct marketing, I studied it because that was the only thing you could
study back in 1996. There was no one teaching how to build your online business,
how to market online, anything like that, so I went back old-school, and I started
studying some of the old-school masters. There's been stuff since the 1920s that you
could study about old- school direct marketing. That's where my first leanings and
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
education was.
There are three core fundamentals in direct marketing:
the list, the offer, and the copy.
Three big sections in direct marketing.
The list is basically the list ... and back then it would be like direct mail
usually, or you might be advertising in a magazine or a newspaper. But a lot of times
just direct mail, it would be the list that you were mailing. A lot of times you would
rent a list or buy a list and send that direct mail. You would always have to worry
about getting the right list because if you had a list of people that were interested in
fly-fishing, and you tried to sell them something about playing guitar, you probably
were not going to get great results because some of those fly-fisher people are
probably interested in guitar, but not many of them, not all of them. You had to get
the right list.
The offer is what you are actually offering them. What are you going to give
them? What do they get when they place an order with you, when they buy your
stuff?
Then copy is the sales copy. Back then it was usually direct marketing, like
sales letters. It would be like printed out and sent to them, or part of an
advertisement.
There was list, offer, and copy. The way it worked is, you could actually not
get one of these right (you could sort of screw up on one of these) and still be OK. If
you had the right list and the right offer, but you didn't have great copy, you'd still be
OK. Or if you had a lousy offer, but you had the right list, and you had great copy,
you'd probably be OK.
You might get a lot of returns, but you'd probably make a lot of sales. Then if
you didn't really have a list, or if you just had a general list and it wasn't very
targeted, but you had an incredible offer and an incredible copy, then you'd be OK.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
To give an example, Gary Halbert, a legendary direct-marketing copywriter,
passed away several years ago. I actually got to meet Gary on a few occasions and go
out to dinner with him once. That was really cool, he’s a brilliant genius. He had a
great example: if you had a hamburger stand, what is the one thing that you would
want more than anything else in your marketing?
He said, "Well, you have a hamburger stand, but you could take all the copy in
the world, and throw it away. If you had a crowd of starving people, it doesn't matter.
It doesn't really matter how good your food was. It doesn't matter how you advertised
it, what the copy was. As long as you had a starving audience you're going to sell a lot
of hamburgers." Right?
You have the right offer at the right time. On the other hand, if you have some
people that are just mildly hungry, but you have a fantastic offer (as in you have free
hamburgers, and you advertise it in a really great way) you’re probably going to be
able to either give away or sell a bunch of food, OK?
The final one was if you had hungry people, and you had a bunch of burgers,
you probably don't need good copy. You can see how you can drop one of those three
out of there, and you'd still be OK. Now, if you get all three of them working,
then you win. That's when you're going to have this massive, massive success.
That's old-school marketing. Now, the interesting thing in our world with
Product Launch Formula is, we've changed this around a little bit because you don't
really need a list, because you're going to be building your own. Maybe down the
road, you'll start advertising. You can start buying lists, sort of that old-school way,
and there are ways to do that ethically.
What you're going to be doing is you're going to be building a
business where the focus of the business is to continue to build your list,
and one of the best ways to do it is with Product Launch Formula.
In terms of the offer, because of the launch conversation you're going to have
(which you’ll learn inside Product Launch Formula), as you release Prelaunch
Content the market is going to tell you what your offer should be. That's
the beautiful thing.
Because of the sequential nature of your launch, you're not depending on one
great sales letter, so you're able to fine-tune your copy as you go through your launch.
These three things almost build themselves for you as you go through your launch,
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Now, I do have another diagram for you that I want to draw real quick. These

PLF really does come down to stories, sequences, and triggers,

going start off with this initial piece (which is sort of like your fan base) that first
circle. Then the second circle, it just keeps on growing and growing because you start
to build your client list. Then beyond that there is the much bigger list - your
prospect list.
These continually expand as you stack your launches, as you go from one
launch to another launch. I know this is super conceptual right now, but this is the
core of PLF. It isn't like you're focused with old-school direct marketing, where, "I've
got to go buy this list." What you're doing is you're putting the stories, triggers, and
sequences into your marketing, and that will make you attractive, as you start to
share this stuff, and it just naturally expands your markets.
Now, down the road, you might put together a great launch, and you get into

business here. Initially just through word of mouth, you're going to build up these
inner two circles. That's sort of, in a nutshell, Product Launch Formula.

With product - it's the Seed LaunchTM - that’s how you build great products.

As we wrap this up, there's two things that will hold you back in this program.
The first one is "I knew that." Please don't fall into looking and saying, "I
already knew that. Just go on. I don't have to ... Whatever, Jeff. Move on." Don't do
that. Don't just tune out and say, "I already knew that," because a lot of these things
I'm doing, they might look like you've seen this before, but I have major twists on
almost everything. "Don't be too cool for school." Love that saying.
I recently spoke with Ryan Deiss, who is an incredible marketer and someone
I've known since he was in college, just incredible business entrepreneur, and he
said, "Jeff, I need to get into PLF," so I set him up with PLF, and he went through
every single video in the entire course. Then he did a $3.6 million launch. This guy is
one of the best marketers in the world I know. He went through every single
video.
Stu McLaren, another great marketer I know, did the same thing. He recently
did a launch. He went back and went through every single video even though he's
done dozens of launches.
If those guys can go through it, you can go through it. Don't be too cool for
school. Just turn off that "I knew that" thing.
The second thing is “I disagree.” “Jeff, I've seen that, and that isn't how
it worked for me. That's not going to work for me. I disagree." Well, I've got some
skills here. I've been doing this stuff for a long time. Just follow the plan. The plan
works.
Those are the two things. Just try to turn those off. A lot of us have got that in
our brain. We just automatically turn to, “I disagree" or, "I knew that." Those two
things will hold you back.
Finally, community - in Product Launch Formula, community is
absolutely key. Jumping in and taking part in the community will drive you
further, farther, faster.
Getting involved, getting support, giving support is the way you win.
